title: carex digitata subsp. ornithopoda
intro: Carex digitata subsp. ornithopoda, also known as the ornithopod sedge, is a subspecies of the Carex digitata plant, a type of grass-like flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The ornithopod sedge has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect wounds. The health benefits of the ornithopod sedge include its ability to reduce swelling and promote healing, as well as its potential to help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and bloating. In herbal preparations, the ornithopod sedge is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a range of health benefits.
